title: Ship v2.0.0 — the library has never been released and its own quickstart 404s
description: git tag -l is EMPTY. platform-library/Chart.yaml:5 says version 2.0.0, but CHANGELOG.md:10 still says '## [Unreleased]'. release.yaml:47-54 gates on a literal '## [2.0.0]' heading, so a v2.0.0 tag pushed today would FAIL the project's own release gate. Consequence: nothing has ever been pushed to oci://ghcr.io/caretak3r/charts, so README.md:43-51's headline quickstart (scaffold --repo oci://... then helm dependency update) returns 404 for every external consumer, as does site/docs/getting-started/index.md:19 and every dependency snippet. Every fixture and the scaffolder default (new-app-chart.sh:30) use file://../platform-library, so the OCI consumption path this library exists to provide has never once been exercised end to end. A common library nobody can helm pull is a library in name only.
design: Sequence: (1) roll CHANGELOG [Unreleased] into a dated ## [2.0.0] heading; (2) fix Chart.yaml:17 home URL (currently github.com/rohit/helm-factory — a nonexistent repo — vs caretak3r everywhere else) so the published chart metadata and Artifact Hub are not wrong on day one; (3) enable GitHub Pages (Settings -> Pages -> Source: GitHub Actions), which AGENTS.md:63-65 notes is still not switched on, so README.md:14's 'Full docs' link resolves; (4) push v2.0.0; (5) VERIFY as a real consumer would — scaffold a throwaway chart against oci://ghcr.io/caretak3r/charts and helm dependency update it from OUTSIDE the repo. Step 5 is the point; do not close this on a green workflow alone.
acceptance_criteria: v2.0.0 tag exists and the release workflow went green; 'helm pull oci://ghcr.io/caretak3r/charts/platform --version 2.0.0' succeeds from a clean directory outside the repo; a scaffolded chart with --repo oci://ghcr.io/caretak3r/charts resolves via helm dependency update; the docs site loads at the README's advertised URL.

title: Service selector includes mutable commonLabels — changing them orphans the Service
description: platform-library/templates/_service.yaml:51-55 builds the Service spec.selector from platform.selectorLabels PLUS every entry in .Values.commonLabels. Kubernetes workload selectors are immutable and pod labels only change on rollout; if a consumer changes or removes a commonLabels entry, the Service selector changes while existing pods may not match it, so the Service silently stops selecting any endpoints — traffic blackholes with no render-time error. Production-incident class, not a doc footnote. Also listed in CORE.md 'Known Issues (Tracked)'.
design: Build the Service selector from stable selectorLabels only: delete the commonLabels range at _service.yaml:53-55 (keep commonLabels on metadata.labels, which is safe and mutable). NOTE: this changes rendered selector output for existing consumers, so it is a breaking change to rendered manifests — needs a major version bump per README 'Releasing' (major for any breaking values/template change) plus a migration note (pods keep matching because selectorLabels are a subset of pod labels).
acceptance_criteria: Service selector renders only platform.selectorLabels; golden snapshots updated; CHANGELOG + migration note explain the selector change; CORE.md Known Issues row removed.
close_reason: Fixed by the component-label refactor: platform.selectorLabels now emits name+instance+component and is built only from stable chart-derived values; commonLabels removed from the Service and ServiceMonitor selectors; CronJob pods are component=cronjob and hook-Job pods component=preinstall/postinstall, so the Service and PDB no longer match them. New 'selector stability' gate in lint-library.sh mutation-tested to catch regressions. Goldens regenerated; full gate PASS.

title: P0-5: image.tag defaults to latest — require explicit tag/digest
description: values.yaml:38 and :296 (jobs.image.tag) default latest, plus fallback in _helpers.tpl:63. Fix: default tag empty and fail with clear required message when neither tag nor digest set; document digest as preferred pin. (fable5-review.md #5)
close_reason: Completed TLS self-signed Secret lookup reuse and explicit image tag/digest enforcement with validation.

title: P0-3: Self-signed TLS regenerates CA/key on every render — add lookup reuse
description: _tls-selfsigned.yaml:8-9 calls genCA/genSignedCert unconditionally; every helm upgrade rotates CA+key. Fix: lookup existing <fullname>-tls Secret and reuse tls.crt/tls.key when present; regenerate only when absent/near expiry. Mark tlsSelfSigned dev-only in README; steer prod to cert-manager certificate. (fable5-review.md #3)
close_reason: Completed TLS self-signed Secret lookup reuse and explicit image tag/digest enforcement with validation.

title: P0-4: Enforce values schema (publish values.schema.json + lint fixtures)
description: Only values.schema.reference.json exists; Helm only enforces values.schema.json. Fix: publish/copy reference as values.schema.json in generated charts; add lint step validating each test fixture's effective root values (check-jsonschema); tighten schema: enums for image.pullPolicy/service.type/mtls.policy/workload.type, reject image.tag latest, require parentRefs when gateway route enabled. (fable5-review.md #4)
close_reason: Implemented via relay on relay/6d1fa3a-schema-scaffold, judge PASS 0.94

title: P0-1: Enable securityContext defaults (PSS-restricted)
description: values.yaml:421-433 podSecurityContext.enabled and containerSecurityContext.enabled are false, so hardened sub-values are dead code (gates at _helpers.tpl:200-210). Fix: flip both enabled to true; add seccompProfile RuntimeDefault to both; add runAsNonRoot true to podSecurityContext; flip readOnlyRootFilesystem to true; document PSS-restricted as the target profile. (fable5-review.md #1)
close_reason: Implemented via relay on relay/b8d109f-p0-security-defaults, judge PASS 0.94

title: P0-2: Harden hook Jobs and CronJobs (securityContext, resources, pullSecrets)
description: platform.renderHookJob (_helpers.tpl ~662-729) emits no pod/container securityContext; _cronjob.yaml has no securityContext anywhere, fallback container has no securityContext/resources, pull secrets ignore image.pullSecrets. Fix: apply podSecurityContext/containerSecurityContext defaults and jobs.resources to hook Job + CronJob pods; merge image.pullSecrets into CronJob pull secrets. (fable5-review.md #2)
close_reason: Implemented via relay on relay/b8d109f-p0-security-defaults, judge PASS 0.94

title: Commit v2 branch as stable base
description: fable5-review.md repo-state note: entire v2 refactor is uncommitted on feat/platform-library-v2-capability-gates (_capabilities.tpl, _util.tpl, scripts/, tests/, docs untracked; 25 non-underscore templates staged as deleted; core templates modified). Commit everything so all fixes have a stable base.
close_reason: Committed as ef48069 on feat/platform-library-v2-capability-gates

title: serviceEndpoints.enabled emits nonsense — treats every values block as a subchart; delete the feature
description: Reproduced with helm template on the minimal fixture: 'helm template t . --set serviceEndpoints.enabled=true' emits a ConfigMap containing 'podSecurityContext-endpoint: podSecurityContext.default.svc.cluster.local:80', 'affinity-endpoint: affinity.default.svc.cluster.local:80', 'containerSecurityContext-endpoint: ...' and an 'enabled-subcharts' list naming every defaults block in values.yaml. Root cause: global.enabledSubcharts / global.allEndpointsDynamic (_helpers.tpl:512-555) range over .Values and treat every map-valued key as a subchart, excluding only global/nameOverride/common. That heuristic was coherent under v1's nested-subchart model; under v2's flattened 'import-values: [defaults]' contract every defaults block looks like a subchart, so the feature is structurally broken, not merely buggy. It is wired into _app.yaml:94 and has zero fixture coverage — vault/concepts/thin-ice-untested-surfaces.md:5 already admits this surface is untested.
design: Delete rather than repair. The feature cannot be made correct without a registry of real subcharts, which v2's flattened contract does not provide, and no consumer can be relying on output this broken. Remove: serviceEndpoints from values.yaml + schema, platform.serviceEndpoints.configmap, the _app.yaml:94 dispatch, and the dead helpers global.enabledSubcharts / global.allEndpointsDynamic / global.allEndpoints / platform.service.endpoint / global.subchartEndpoint (all confirmed to have zero other callers). If cross-service discovery is genuinely wanted later, reintroduce it as an explicit user-supplied map, not an inferred one.
acceptance_criteria: serviceEndpoints removed from values.yaml, schema, _app.yaml and _helpers.tpl; the five dead helpers deleted; full render matrix and goldens still pass; CHANGELOG records the removal as breaking (safe under the unreleased 2.0.0).
close_reason: serviceEndpoints + 5 zero-caller helpers + platform.util.merge + 3 dead values keys removed; PDB maxUnavailable made reachable; 3 undeclared keys declared. Goldens byte-identical across all 4 fixtures, proving the code was dead. Full gate PASS (REQUIRE_KUBECONFORM=1 REQUIRE_CHECK_JSONSCHEMA=1).

title: updateStrategy Recreate/OnDelete still emits rollingUpdate — API server rejects the object
description: _deployment.yaml:35-37 does a wholesale 'toYaml .Values.updateStrategy', and values.yaml:110-114 ships rollingUpdate.maxSurge/maxUnavailable as defaults. Setting updateStrategy.type: Recreate therefore renders BOTH keys: 'strategy: {rollingUpdate: {maxSurge: 25%, maxUnavailable: 25%}, type: Recreate}', which the API server rejects with 'may not be specified when strategy type is Recreate'. helm template passes; helm install fails. Same class of bug in _statefulset.yaml:30-32 (type: OnDelete retains rollingUpdate.partition, values.yaml:436-439) and _daemonset.yaml:31-33. This is a values-shape landmine: the consumer sets one documented key and gets an object that cannot be applied.
design: Strip the incompatible sub-key at render time based on .type rather than trusting toYaml: when type is Recreate (Deployment) or OnDelete (StatefulSet/DaemonSet), omit rollingUpdate entirely. Prefer this over documenting 'also null out rollingUpdate yourself', which pushes a Kubernetes API invariant onto the consumer — the whole point of the library is to know these rules for them.
acceptance_criteria: --set updateStrategy.type=Recreate renders a Deployment with type: Recreate and NO rollingUpdate key; same for StatefulSet OnDelete (no partition) and DaemonSet OnDelete; kubeconform passes on all three; negative assertions added to lint-library.sh.

title: Sidecars, initContainers and cronJob containers bypass containerSecurityContext — PSS-restricted default is false for them
description: values.yaml:476-482 claims the library ships PSS-restricted by default. That is only true for the ONE library-managed app container. platform.podTemplateSpec toYaml's user containers verbatim: initContainers.containers (_helpers.tpl:211), sidecars.containers (_helpers.tpl:283), cronJob.containers (_cronjob.yaml:75), and hook-job sidecars (_helpers.tpl:707) receive NO containerSecurityContext injection, no platform.image resolution, and no imagePullPolicy. A sidecar therefore runs as root with allowPrivilegeEscalation unset while the main container is hardened — silently defeating the library's headline security claim, and defeating PSS-restricted admission for the whole pod (which is evaluated per-container).
design: Two options. (a) Merge the library's containerSecurityContext into each user-supplied container as a DEFAULT, letting the user's own securityContext keys win on conflict — preserves the escape hatch while making the safe path the default. (b) Fail closed when a user container omits securityContext and containerSecurityContext.enabled is true, forcing an explicit opt-out. (a) is recommended: it matches the library's existing 'defaults move, flags stay' philosophy and does not break existing consumers. Whichever is chosen, apply it uniformly across all four passthrough sites.
acceptance_criteria: A fixture with a sidecar that sets no securityContext renders that sidecar WITH runAsNonRoot/allowPrivilegeEscalation:false/capabilities.drop[ALL]/seccompProfile; a sidecar that DOES set its own securityContext keeps its own values; same verified for initContainers, cronJob.containers, and hook-job sidecars; golden updated; a lint-library.sh posture assertion proves a bare sidecar cannot render unhardened.

title: lint-library.sh kubeconform validates only the 1.31 render against every version in the matrix
description: scripts/lint-library.sh renders each fixture once at GOLDEN_KUBE_VERSION=1.31 into $raw (:129) and the kubeconform loop (:150-161) feeds that same 1.31 output to kubeconform -kubernetes-version $kv.0 for every version 1.31-1.36. The library's core behavior is per-version apiVersion negotiation, so the manifests actually produced at 1.32-1.36 are never schema-validated — the gate advertises matrix validation it doesn't perform. Distinct from the tracked golden-snapshots-only-at-1.31 bead: that is snapshot coverage; this is schema-validating the wrong render. Verified by direct read (the render matrix loop earlier in the script only checks render success per version, not conformance).
design: Inside the kubeconform loop, render the fixture at each $kv (tests/render.sh already accepts --kube-version) and pipe THAT version's output to kubeconform with the matching -kubernetes-version; or restructure to a single loop that renders once per version and does render-success + kubeconform together (halves the renders vs today's two loops). Watch CI time: 4 fixtures x 6 versions kubeconform runs already happen — only the rendered input changes, so cost is ~24 extra helm template calls (~seconds). May surface real pre-existing schema violations at 1.32-1.36; fix those as part of this bead or file follow-ups.
acceptance_criteria: Each fixture is kubeconform-validated against version X using the manifests rendered AT version X for all supported versions; CI green; any newly surfaced conformance failures resolved or tracked.
close_reason: Fixed by d1a9653 — lint-library.sh now renders and validates per-version; kubeconform runs against each k8s version's own render, not the floor render.

title: Pre-install hook Job references ConfigMap/ServiceAccount that don't exist yet on fresh install
description: platform-library/templates/_helpers.tpl:723 annotates hook Jobs with helm.sh/hook: pre-install,pre-upgrade, but the Job mounts the script ConfigMap '<fullname>-preinstall-script' (_helpers.tpl:663, created by platform.configmap.script in _configmap-script.yaml which has NO hook annotations — it renders as a normal resource) and sets serviceAccountName to the release-managed SA (_helpers.tpl:737, also a normal resource). Helm runs pre-install hooks BEFORE creating normal resources, so on a fresh 'helm install' with jobs.preInstall enabled + a script, the hook pod cannot mount its volume (ConfigMap missing) and may be rejected for a missing SA — install hangs then fails. Invisible to CI because 'helm template' and goldens never execute hooks. postInstall is unaffected (post-install hooks run after normal resources).
design: Fix options (pick one): (a) annotate the preinstall script ConfigMap with helm.sh/hook: pre-install,pre-upgrade and helm.sh/hook-weight lower than the Job's (Job weight is -5 for preinstall; give the ConfigMap e.g. -10), plus hook-delete-policy before-hook-creation so upgrades refresh it; when serviceAccount.create is true also emit a hook-annotated copy of the SA (or simpler: omit serviceAccountName on preinstall Jobs when the SA is release-managed and automount is false anyway); (b) document that preinstall script jobs require an existingServiceAccount and pre-existing ConfigMap — weaker. Prefer (a). Add a negative/behavioral note to tests: template-level tests cannot catch this; at minimum assert the script ConfigMap carries hook annotations when jobs.preInstall uses script/scriptFile. Verify with a kind cluster or 'helm install --dry-run=server' if available.
acceptance_criteria: Fresh helm install with jobs.preInstall.script set succeeds; script ConfigMap renders with pre-install hook annotations and lower weight than the Job; goldens updated; lint-library.sh asserts hook annotations on the script ConfigMap.

title: Warn in NOTES.txt when enabled Kinds are silently skipped by capability gating
description: When a CRD-backed object (Certificate, HTTPRoute/GRPCRoute, ServiceMonitor/PodMonitor, Istio mTLS objects, etc.) is enabled in values but skipped because its apiVersion is not served and not force-assumed (platform-library/templates/_capabilities.tpl gating), the chart renders nothing for that Kind and nothing tells the operator. An operator can believe cert-manager Certificates or monitors are deployed when they are not — a silent security/observability gap.
design: Extend platform.notes in platform-library/templates/_notes.tpl (34 lines today): for each feature that is enabled in values, re-run the same capability check the emitter uses (the Kind→apiVersion negotiation in _capabilities.tpl) and append a WARNING listing every enabled-but-skipped Kind with the apiVersions that were tried and a pointer to the force-assume mechanism (global.capabilities / --api-versions). NOTES.txt content does not appear in helm template manifest output, so golden snapshots and kubeconform are unaffected. Consider a small shared helper 'platform.capabilities.skippedKinds' so notes and emitters stay in sync.
acceptance_criteria: Enabling e.g. certificate.enabled=true without cert-manager.io/v1 served produces a NOTES.txt WARNING naming Certificate; no warning when the apiVersion is served or force-assumed; golden snapshots unchanged.

title: P1-9: Guardrails for extraManifests/extraObjects/sidecar escape hatches
description: extraManifests tpl with full root context (_util.tpl:114-115); extraObjects renders any Kind incl cluster-scoped RBAC (_util.tpl:46-103, _capabilities.tpl:222); sidecars/initContainers/extraVolumes verbatim. Fix: document trust model; add allowClusterScopedExtras:false gate for cluster-scoped Kinds in extraObjects; NOTES.txt/fail warnings for hostPath/privileged/cluster RBAC in extras. (fable5-review.md #9)
close_reason: Implemented via relay on relay/e8abfe4-p1-posture, judge PASS 0.95

title: P1-10: Network exposure defaults (ingress.tls, NetworkPolicy default-deny footgun)
description: networkPolicy.enabled false (values.yaml:506), ingress.tls false (values.yaml:212); enabling NetworkPolicy with empty rules (_networkpolicy.yaml:26-31) silently default-denies. Fix: default ingress.tls true when hostname set; document default-deny+allowlist baseline; NOTES.txt warning when NetworkPolicy enabled with empty rules. (fable5-review.md #10)
close_reason: Implemented via relay on relay/e8abfe4-p1-posture, judge PASS 0.95

title: P1-7: mTLS AuthorizationPolicy fail-closed (empty allowedPrincipals)
description: values.yaml:278-279 defaults allowedPrincipals to cluster.local/ns/*/sa/* rendered into ALLOW policy (_mtls.yaml:29-33) — no meaningful authz. Fix: default allowedPrincipals []; fail with helpful message (or skip policy) unless principals listed; offer same-namespace default as easy opt-in. (fable5-review.md #7)
close_reason: Implemented via relay on relay/e8abfe4-p1-posture, judge PASS 0.95

title: P1-8: existingSecret pattern + secrets-in-values guidance
description: secret.stringData (values.yaml:191, _secret.yaml:33-34) and raw cert/key in _tls-secrets.yaml:22-23 steer users to plaintext secrets in git. Fix: first-class existingSecret reference everywhere a secret is consumed; document External Secrets/SealedSecrets as prod path; README warning on secret.stringData. (fable5-review.md #8)
close_reason: Implemented via relay on relay/e8abfe4-p1-posture, judge PASS 0.95

title: P1-6: ServiceAccount defaults — dedicated SA, render automountServiceAccountToken, enableServiceLinks false
description: values.yaml:410-414 serviceAccount.create false; automountServiceAccountToken declared but never rendered (no-op). Fix: render automountServiceAccountToken on SA + pod spec, default false; default serviceAccount.create true; set enableServiceLinks false in pod spec. (fable5-review.md #6)
close_reason: Implemented via relay on relay/b8d109f-p0-security-defaults, judge PASS 0.94

title: No first-class RBAC generator — the library creates a ServiceAccount per app and gives it no way to grant it anything
description: The library defaults serviceAccount.create: true (values.yaml:467), giving every app a dedicated identity — then offers no Role/RoleBinding generator. Any app that actually needs to call the Kubernetes API must set automountServiceAccountToken: true and hand-roll extraObjects.Role + extraObjects.RoleBinding, writing its own roleRef and subjects and keeping the subject name in sync with platform.serviceAccountName by hand. For a library whose entire pitch is 'set values, get correct manifests', dedicated-identity-with-no-permissions is a half-built feature: it is the single most common thing a platform library is asked for after the workload itself. Related registry-only Kinds with no generator, same shape of gap: PrometheusRule and Probe (monitors have generators, alerting rules do not), cert-manager Issuer/ClusterIssuer (certificate.issuer references an issuer the library cannot create), Gateway/ReferenceGrant (parentRefs is REQUIRED at _gateway-api.yaml:36 but must point at a Gateway someone else made; cross-namespace backendRefs need a ReferenceGrant the library will not emit).
design: Add rbac.enabled with rbac.rules (a plain list of policy rules) generating a Role + RoleBinding bound to the chart's own ServiceAccount, with the subject name wired to platform.serviceAccountName so it cannot drift. Keep it namespaced-only by default; cluster-scoped RBAC stays behind the existing allowClusterScopedExtras gate (_util.tpl:98), which already exists precisely for this. Follow .claude/skills/add-library-kind for the registry/schema/fixture checklist.
acceptance_criteria: rbac.enabled: true with a rules list renders a Role and a RoleBinding correctly bound to the chart's ServiceAccount; the binding survives a serviceAccount.name override; schema constrains rules shape; fixture + golden cover it; security-posture-invariants reviewed (this grants permissions — the guardrails matter).

title: Local lint gate is weaker than CI — add a Makefile and required-by-default checks so contributors cannot go green locally and red in CI
description: scripts/lint-library.sh silently downgrades its two strongest gates unless env vars are set: kubeconform (lint-library.sh:91, 'WARN ... SKIPPED') and check-jsonschema (:101) both default to OFF locally, and are only forced on by REQUIRE_KUBECONFORM=1 / REQUIRE_CHECK_JSONSCHEMA=1 in ci.yaml:46 and release.yaml:67. A contributor with neither tool installed gets a clean pass locally, then a red CI. Compounding it: there is no Makefile, justfile, .tool-versions, .mise.toml, or devcontainer anywhere in the repo, so to reproduce CI you must reverse-engineer the workflow to discover you need Helm exactly 4.2.0, kubeconform v0.8.0, check-jsonschema, shellcheck, and jq. Nothing tells you this. Plan item TEST-2 covers the first half.
design: (1) Flip lint-library.sh's own defaults to required, with an actionable 'install X: <link>' message and an explicit opt-out env var for genuinely tool-less environments — the inverse of today's fail-open default. (2) Add a Makefile with 'make lint' (the exact CI invocation), 'make render', 'make golden' (UPDATE_GOLDEN=1), and 'make tools' (or at minimum a documented tool list with pinned versions in one place, since ci.yaml and release.yaml currently duplicate the install blocks with only a comment as the sync mechanism — release.yaml:21). (3) While in there: lint-library.sh:353 still swallows stderr from helm dependency update, the exact bug fable5-review.md:146 flagged and render.sh:14 already fixed.
acceptance_criteria: A fresh clone with no tools installed fails 'make lint' with a message naming every missing tool and how to install it; 'make lint' runs byte-identically to what CI runs; tool versions are declared in exactly one place.

title: Docs teach the bare --api-versions form that silently skips objects (site + CORE.md + render.sh)
description: README.md:955 correctly warns that --api-versions requires the full group/version/Kind form and that a bare 'group/version' does NOT satisfy the capability gate — the object is skipped SILENTLY. Commit 938d0c9 fixed the README and nothing else. Three places still teach the broken form: site/docs/capability-catalog/index.md:39-40 ('Equivalently, pass helm template --api-versions <group/version>' — directly contradicting the README), CORE.md:284 ('tests/render.sh full --kube-version 1.34 --api-versions cert-manager.io/v1'), and tests/render.sh:5's usage comment ('--api-versions rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1'). The failure mode is a silent skip, which is the worst possible one: the user follows the docs, sees a clean exit 0, and never learns their object vanished. The published docs site is the copy most new users will read.
acceptance_criteria: All three locations use the full group/version/Kind form; a grep for '--api-versions' across the repo shows no bare group/version examples; the capability-catalog page explains WHY the bare form fails rather than just showing the right one.

title: gatewayApi.apiVersion hardcoded default defeats negotiation and can emit an unserved apiVersion
description: values.yaml:245 ships a NON-EMPTY default 'apiVersion: gateway.networking.k8s.io/v1', and _gateway-api.yaml:15 resolves as 'default (negotiated) $gw.apiVersion' — so the consumer-facing default always wins and the negotiated value is discarded. Every other CRD-backed generator (Certificate values.yaml:253, monitors :266) correctly ships apiVersion: "" and lets _capabilities.tpl negotiate. Verified failure mode: on a cluster serving only gateway.networking.k8s.io/v1beta1, the capability gate at _app.yaml:58 PASSES (it negotiates v1beta1 successfully) and the object then renders as apiVersion: .../v1 — a version the cluster does not serve. The v1beta1 fallback at _capabilities.tpl:147 is therefore unreachable. This is plan item CAP-7, and it is the one Kind where negotiation is silently switched off.
design: Default gatewayApi.apiVersion to "" so negotiation runs, matching every other CRD-backed generator. Note this changes rendered output for consumers on pre-1.0 Gateway API installs — from a hard apiVersion mismatch to a correct v1beta1 render, i.e. strictly an improvement, but it IS a behavior change and plan Decision #4 (productionization-plan.md:391) flags it as needing sign-off. Safe to land inside the unreleased 2.0.0.
acceptance_criteria: With only gateway.networking.k8s.io/v1beta1 forced via --api-versions, the HTTPRoute renders as v1beta1, not v1; with v1 available it still renders v1; goldens updated; a render-matrix leg covers the v1beta1 negotiation path (today no test exercises it).

title: Cross-field guards missing: ExternalName Service, certificate.issuer, and ingress-without-service all render invalid or dangling objects
description: Three separate missing guards, all the same shape — the library renders something the API server or the cluster will reject, when a fail() at template time would have caught it. (1) service.type: ExternalName is permitted by the schema (values.schema.reference.json:35) but _service.yaml never emits spec.externalName, so the object renders with ports+selector and no externalName and is rejected ('spec.externalName: Required value'). (2) certificate.issuer defaults to "" (values.yaml:281) and _certificate.yaml:33 renders 'name:' (null) with no required/fail — unlike mtls (_mtls.yaml:8) and image (_helpers.tpl:68), which both fail properly. The schema's if/then (values.schema.reference.json:82-83) only catches it IF the consumer's copied values.schema.json is in sync, which is itself an open issue (hf-th2). (3) ingress.enabled: true with service.enabled: false (the DEFAULT, values.yaml:67) renders an Ingress whose backend points at a Service that does not exist — platform.primaryServicePort (_helpers.tpl:105) and the backends at _ingress.yaml:54 / _gateway-api.yaml:7 are computed without consulting service.enabled.
design: Add three fail() guards in the library's established style (see the nine existing guards — their error messages are genuinely good; match that tone: state what collided, why it cannot work, and the exact key to change). For ExternalName specifically, decide whether to support it properly (emit spec.externalName, omit ports/selector) or reject it in the schema — supporting it is little extra work and the schema already advertises it.
acceptance_criteria: Each of the three combinations fails at helm template time with an actionable message naming the offending key; three matching negative assertions in lint-library.sh; the ExternalName path either renders a valid Service or is removed from the schema enum.

title: extraObjects: surface unknown Kinds instead of silently dropping them (PRD open question)
description: An extraObjects entry whose Kind has no registry entry and no explicit apiVersion negotiates to empty and is silently skipped (_util.tpl genericResource: apiVersionFor returns "" → 'if $api' wraps all output → nothing rendered, no signal). A typo'd Kind ('Configmap') vanishes identically to a legitimately capability-skipped CRD. docs/prd/platform-library-v2.md:179-180 explicitly lists this as the open design question: reject unknown Kinds with a helpful fail, or stay permissive.
design: Distinguish the two silent cases in genericResource/_util.tpl: (a) Kind in registry but no served/assumed apiVersion → legitimate capability skip (pairs with the tracked NOTES.txt skipped-Kinds warning bead — share the collection mechanism); (b) Kind NOT in registry AND no explicit apiVersion → almost certainly an error: fail with 'extraObjects Kind %q is not in the capability registry and has no explicit apiVersion — set apiVersion on the object or check the Kind spelling'. Failing on (b) preserves the documented flexibility (any Kind renders if you give it an apiVersion) while killing the typo footgun; ship as fail (render-time, caught in CI) rather than warning, with a values escape hatch only if a real use case emerges. Add negative assertion to lint-library.sh (unknown Kind without apiVersion must fail) and a positive one (unknown Kind WITH explicit apiVersion renders).
acceptance_criteria: Typo'd Kind without apiVersion fails render with actionable message; unknown Kind with explicit apiVersion still renders; capability-skipped registry Kinds unaffected; assertions added; PRD open question resolved and noted.

title: Close template test-coverage gaps: GRPCRoute, rollout checksums, HPA/NetworkPolicy branches, fail guards, native --api-versions negotiation
description: Fixture/golden coverage misses whole feature paths (verified by grep over tests/fixtures + tests/golden): (1) GRPCRoute — ~70 lines in _gateway-api.yaml:108-178 never rendered by any fixture; no golden contains kind: GRPCRoute. (2) Rollout checksum annotations (_helpers.tpl:454-466) appear in no golden: full fixture is a Deployment without configMap/secret; stateful enables both but is a StatefulSet (Deployment-only gate — see the rollout-annotations bead). (3) HPA advanced branches (autoscaling.behavior/targetMemory/custom metrics, _helpers.tpl:409-433) and NetworkPolicy rule branches (_networkpolicy.yaml:14-31) gated on but never populated — full fixture snapshots only an empty default-deny policy and CPU-only HPA. (4) Three fail guards untested: _gateway-api.yaml:36 and :119 (route enabled without parentRefs) and _configmap-script.yaml:41 (script file not found) — lint-library.sh asserts 5 of 8 fail paths. (5) Capability negotiation is only ever exercised via the force-assume values list; the native Capabilities.APIVersions.Has branch (_capabilities.tpl:30-35) is never proven with real helm --api-versions flags. Long tail (lower value): sessionAffinity/externalTrafficPolicy/loadBalancerSourceRanges, jobs.postInstall hook variant, persistence existingClaim/dataSource. Known structural limitation to document, not fix: the tls-selfsigned lookup-reuse branch can't run offline (lookup empty under helm template).
design: Single PR touching only tests/ + scripts/lint-library.sh + goldens: (a) enable gatewayApi.grpcRoute with parentRefs in the full fixture, bump expected_kinds full count (lint-library.sh:44); (b) enable configMap on full so Deployment checksums land in a golden, assert checksum/config via grep in lint-library.sh; (c) add autoscaling.behavior + targetMemory + one custom metric and one networkPolicy ingress+egress rule to full; (d) add three negative-render blocks mirroring the existing 'render 2>&1 | grep -q <msg>' idiom for the untested fail guards; (e) add one positive native-negotiation render: render.sh full --set capabilities.apiVersions=null --api-versions gateway.networking.k8s.io/v1 (plus cert-manager.io/v1 etc.) asserting HTTPRoute renders WITHOUT force-assume; (f) fold in cheap long-tail keys (postInstall, sessionAffinity, existingClaim) where they fit existing fixtures; document the lookup-reuse offline limitation in CORE.md. Regenerate goldens (UPDATE_GOLDEN=1) once at the end; kubeconform then validates the richer objects.
acceptance_criteria: GRPCRoute, checksum annotations, HPA behavior/memory/custom-metric, and populated NetworkPolicy rules all appear in committed goldens; all 8 fail guards have negative assertions; at least one CRD group proves native --api-versions negotiation without force-assume; matrix still green.

title: lint-library.sh negative-render assignment unguarded — a render failure silently aborts the remaining gate checks
description: scripts/lint-library.sh:166: neg=$("$RENDER" full --set capabilities.apiVersions=null 2>/dev/null) is a bare command-substitution assignment under set -euo pipefail. If render.sh exits non-zero the whole script exits at that line with no message (stderr discarded) and every later check — empty-doc, image-pin enforcement, schema enforcement, posture guardrails (:178-268) — is silently skipped. CI shows a bare failure with no diagnostic; worse, a transient breakage pattern could mask the guardrail suite entirely. Every other check in the script uses the guarded 'if out=$(...); then' idiom.
design: Wrap in the surrounding idiom: if ! neg=$("$RENDER" full --set capabilities.apiVersions=null 2>&1); then echo '  FAIL: negative render itself failed'; echo "$neg" | tail -5; fail=1; else <existing greps>; fi. Keep stderr (drop 2>/dev/null) so the failure is diagnosable. Audit the script for any other bare assignments (grep -n '=$(' and check each against set -e semantics).
acceptance_criteria: A deliberately broken negative render (e.g. temporary syntax error) produces a FAIL message and lets the remaining checks run, exiting 1 at the end; normal runs unchanged.

title: Harden CI/release workflows: job-split token scope, SHA-pin actions, checksum tool installs, concurrency, dedupe
description: Supply-chain gaps in .github/workflows/: (1) release.yaml:8-11 grants packages:write at workflow level to the single job that also curls helm/kubeconform tarballs into the shell (:22-35) and renders attacker-influenceable templates via lint-library.sh (:57-58) — any compromise there runs with GHCR publish rights; checkout defaults persist-credentials:true. (2) actions/checkout pinned by mutable tag @v4 in both workflows. (3) Tool installs have no checksum verification and pipx install check-jsonschema is unpinned (non-reproducible gate). (4) release.yaml has no concurrency guard — two close tag pushes race the OCI push. (5) The install+gate steps are duplicated verbatim between ci.yaml and release.yaml with a keep-in-sync comment (release.yaml:21) — toolchain drift risk.
design: One PR, five slices: (a) split release.yaml into gate job (permissions: contents: read — shellcheck/lint/schema/lint-library) and publish job (needs: gate; permissions: packages: write; only helm registry login + package + push), add persist-credentials: false to all checkouts; (b) pin actions/checkout to a full commit SHA with version comment, add dependabot.yml for github-actions ecosystem; (c) verify sha256 of helm + kubeconform tarballs (pin checksums next to versions) and pin check-jsonschema==<version>; (d) add concurrency {group: release-${{ github.ref }}, cancel-in-progress: false} to release.yaml and a PR-scoped cancel-in-progress group to ci.yaml; (e) extract the shared tool-install + gate steps into a composite action (.github/actions/gate) used by both workflows so versions/checksums live in one place. Verify by pushing a test tag on a fork or workflow_dispatch dry run.
acceptance_criteria: packages:write exists only in the publish job; all actions SHA-pinned; tool downloads checksum-verified and versions pinned; concurrency guards in both workflows; install/gate steps defined once.

title: tlsSelfSigned writes <fullname>-tls but Ingress TLS references <hostname>-tls; ingress.selfSigned and global.storageClass are dead values
description: _tls-selfsigned.yaml:3 creates Secret '<fullname>-tls', but _ingress.yaml:66 defaults the TLS secretName to '<hostname>-tls' (or ingress.existingSecret). Enabling ingress.tls + tlsSelfSigned therefore renders an Ingress pointing at a Secret nothing creates unless hostname == fullname — TLS fails until the user manually sets ingress.existingSecret. Additionally _certificate.yaml:31 also defaults its secretName to '<fullname>-tls', so certificate.enabled + tlsSelfSigned.enabled fight over the same Secret name (helm-owned vs cert-manager-owned). Dead values verified by grep: ingress.selfSigned (values.yaml:230) and global.storageClass (values.yaml:24) are referenced by no template (PVC reads only persistence.storageClass, _pvc.yaml:34).
design: (1) Make the wiring converge: when tlsSelfSigned.enabled and ingress.tls and no ingress.existingSecret, default the Ingress secretName to the self-signed Secret's name (share one helper, e.g. platform.tlsSecretName, used by _tls-selfsigned.yaml, _ingress.yaml and _certificate.yaml). (2) fail (or NOTES-warn) when certificate.enabled AND tlsSelfSigned.enabled both target the same secret. (3) Either wire up or delete ingress.selfSigned and global.storageClass from values.yaml + values.schema.reference.json — dead knobs mislead consumers; deletion is a values-contract change, note in CHANGELOG (minor if the keys were no-ops). Add a lint-library.sh assertion rendering daemon/full with tlsSelfSigned+ingress.tls and asserting the Ingress spec.tls secretName equals the generated Secret's name.
acceptance_criteria: tlsSelfSigned + ingress.tls renders a consistent secret reference with no manual existingSecret; conflicting cert sources fail or warn; no dead values remain in values.yaml/schema; assertions added.

title: StatefulSet serviceName defaults to a Service that may not exist and is not headless
description: _statefulset.yaml:25 defaults serviceName to platform.fullname. service.enabled defaults to false, so by default the governing Service doesn't exist; when enabled it is a ClusterIP VIP, not headless, so per-pod stable DNS (<pod>.<svc>.<ns>) does not resolve — undermining the main StatefulSet guarantee. A consumer CAN set service.clusterIP: None (_service.yaml:34-36) to get a headless Service, but nothing guides or enforces that wiring.
design: Options: (a) when workload.type == StatefulSet and statefulSet.serviceName is unset, render an additional headless Service '<fullname>-headless' (clusterIP: None, same selector/ports) and point serviceName at it — matches Bitnami convention, additive and non-breaking; (b) fail at render when workload.type == StatefulSet unless statefulSet.serviceName is set or service.enabled with service.clusterIP == None — stricter, breaking for current stateful consumers; (c) document the required wiring in README + NOTES.txt warning. Prefer (a) with a NOTES warning fallback. Update the stateful fixture + goldens; kubeconform validates the new Service.
acceptance_criteria: Default StatefulSet render produces a resolvable governing headless Service (or a clear render-time error/warning); stateful golden updated; README StatefulSet section documents the behavior.

title: PDB/Service/PodMonitor selectors also match CronJob and hook-Job pods
description: Hook Job pods carry only platform.selectorLabels (_helpers.tpl:733-734); CronJob pods carry selectorLabels + commonLabels (_cronjob.yaml:36-40); no app.kubernetes.io/component discriminator distinguishes them from main workload pods. Consequences: the PDB (selector = pure selectorLabels, _pdb.yaml:11-13) counts transient Job/CronJob pods toward minAvailable and can miscompute allowed disruptions / block node drains; the Service (selectorLabels + commonLabels, _service.yaml:51-55) can route traffic to CronJob pods; the PodMonitor default selector scrapes them. Latent because helm template never runs pods.
design: Safest fix (selectors on live workloads are immutable): add a distinguishing label (e.g. app.kubernetes.io/component: cron / hook) to CronJob and hook-Job POD templates only, and leave main-workload selectors unchanged — Service/PDB/PodMonitor selectors then no longer match because Job pods... NOTE this alone does NOT fix it (selectors match on subset). Correct approach: main workload pods + all selectors gain app.kubernetes.io/component: app (a selector change → breaking, coordinate with the tracked Service-selector bead in the same major bump), OR document + narrow only the PDB via matchExpressions (component NotIn [cron, hook]) which is non-breaking for Service. Decide during implementation; matchExpressions on PDB + component labels on Job pods is the minimal non-breaking slice.
acceptance_criteria: CronJob/hook pods are excluded from PDB disruption accounting and Service endpoints (verified by label math in rendered manifests + lint-library.sh assertions); any selector change ships in the same major bump as the Service-selector fix.
close_reason: Fixed by the component-label refactor: platform.selectorLabels now emits name+instance+component and is built only from stable chart-derived values; commonLabels removed from the Service and ServiceMonitor selectors; CronJob pods are component=cronjob and hook-Job pods component=preinstall/postinstall, so the Service and PDB no longer match them. New 'selector stability' gate in lint-library.sh mutation-tested to catch regressions. Goldens regenerated; full gate PASS.

title: Config/Secret checksum rollout annotations only applied to Deployments — StatefulSet/DaemonSet pods never auto-roll
description: _helpers.tpl:175 gates platform.deployment.rolloutAnnotations (checksum/config + checksum/secret, defined at _helpers.tpl:454-466) on workload.type == "Deployment". For StatefulSet/DaemonSet consumers, changing configMap.data or secret.data does not alter the pod template, so helm upgrade does not restart pods — they run stale config until manually rolled. StatefulSets are precisely the workloads most likely to mount config. Verified by direct read; no golden contains a checksum/ annotation because no Deployment fixture enables configMap/secret (see the test-coverage bead).
design: Drop the Deployment-only guard at _helpers.tpl:175 so all three workload types get the checksum annotations (rename helper to platform.rolloutAnnotations; keep name as deprecated alias if any consumer includes it directly). The checksums are deterministic (sha256 of rendered manifests) so goldens stay stable. Add fixture coverage: enable configMap on the full (Deployment) fixture and rely on stateful fixture (configMap+secret enabled) to snapshot the StatefulSet path. Regenerate goldens; assert 'checksum/config' present in both goldens in lint-library.sh.
acceptance_criteria: StatefulSet/DaemonSet pod templates carry checksum annotations when configMap/secret enabled; goldens show them; lint-library.sh asserts presence.

title: Ingress/Gateway annotation precedence inverted: commonAnnotations override resource-specific annotations
description: _ingress.yaml:12 and _gateway-api.yaml:19-22,93-96,163-166 build annotations with 'merge (dict) .Values.commonAnnotations .Values.<resource>.annotations'. Sprig merge does NOT overwrite existing keys, so keys from commonAnnotations win over the resource-specific block — a consumer overriding e.g. an ingress-class or timeout annotation per-resource is silently ignored. This is inverted relative to every other template (_service.yaml, _secret.yaml, _configmap.yaml, _pvc.yaml range commonAnnotations first then set specific keys, so specific wins). Verified: sprig merge semantics + direct read.
design: Match the Service/Secret pattern: build a dict, range commonAnnotations into it, then range the resource-specific annotations with set (specific applied last). Or use mergeOverwrite with the specific map last. Apply to _ingress.yaml:12 and all three annotation merges in _gateway-api.yaml. Behavior change only for consumers who set the same key in both places (currently common wins — that is the bug); note in CHANGELOG. Add a lint-library.sh assertion: render full with the same annotation key in commonAnnotations and ingress.annotations and assert the ingress-specific value wins.
acceptance_criteria: Resource-specific annotations win over commonAnnotations on Ingress/HTTPRoute/GRPCRoute/Gateway; assertion added; goldens updated if affected.

title: commonLabels/commonAnnotations applied inconsistently — many Kinds omit them
description: Consumers set commonLabels/commonAnnotations expecting chart-wide metadata (cost allocation, ArgoCD tracking, backup selectors, policy annotations), but coverage is partial. commonAnnotations missing on: CronJob (_cronjob.yaml:13-18), ServiceMonitor (_servicemonitor.yaml:13-18), PodMonitor, Certificate, PDB, NetworkPolicy, mTLS objects, HPA (_helpers.tpl:397-401), ServiceAccount (_helpers.tpl:373-385). commonLabels missing on: Ingress, ServiceMonitor, PodMonitor, Certificate (_certificate.yaml:28-29), PDB (_pdb.yaml:8-9), mTLS (_mtls.yaml:16-17), HPA, ServiceAccount. Contrast: workloads, Service, Secret, ConfigMap, PVC apply both. Verified by direct read of _pdb.yaml/_servicemonitor.yaml/_helpers.tpl.
design: Extract a shared metadata helper (e.g. platform.commonMetadata rendering labels: platform.labels + commonLabels and annotations: commonAnnotations merged with per-resource annotations, specific-wins) and use it in every generator; or minimally add the same commonLabels/commonAnnotations ranges the workload templates use to each omitting template. Careful: do NOT add commonLabels to any selector field (see the tracked Service-selector bead) — metadata only. Goldens will churn; regenerate with UPDATE_GOLDEN=1 and review the diff is metadata-only.
acceptance_criteria: Every rendered Kind carries commonLabels and commonAnnotations in metadata (spot-assert in lint-library.sh for PDB + ServiceMonitor + HPA); selectors unchanged; goldens updated.
close_reason: Fixed by the component-label refactor: platform.selectorLabels now emits name+instance+component and is built only from stable chart-derived values; commonLabels removed from the Service and ServiceMonitor selectors; CronJob pods are component=cronjob and hook-Job pods component=preinstall/postinstall, so the Service and PDB no longer match them. New 'selector stability' gate in lint-library.sh mutation-tested to catch regressions. Goldens regenerated; full gate PASS.

title: Golden snapshots only cover k8s 1.31 while support matrix claims 1.31–1.36
description: tests/golden/ holds one set of snapshots (daemon/full/minimal/stateful) rendered at --kube-version 1.31 (CORE.md:157, tests/render.sh), but README declares support for Kubernetes 1.31–1.36. Version-sensitive output — capability negotiation picking different apiVersions per version — is untested at 1.32–1.36; a regression that only manifests at newer versions would pass CI.
design: Options: (a) per-version golden dirs (tests/golden/<kube-version>/...) for fixtures whose output is version-sensitive, generated by lint-library.sh across the matrix; (b) keep single goldens but add an assertion that renders at 1.31 and 1.36 and diffs — committing goldens only where they differ; (c) document in CORE.md why kubeconform across the version matrix is deemed sufficient (it validates schema conformance per version but not apiVersion selection). Prefer (b) as cheapest meaningful coverage; escalate to (a) only if 1.31 vs 1.36 output actually differs today.
acceptance_criteria: Either CI renders/verifies version-sensitive fixtures at both ends of the support matrix, or CORE.md documents the explicit decision that kubeconform coverage suffices.

title: Chart signing/provenance (cosign) for GHCR releases
description: README 'Releasing' (line ~967) states: 'Chart signing/provenance (cosign) is tracked as future work.' Nothing tracks it. Released charts pushed to GHCR are unsigned — consumers cannot verify provenance/integrity of the OCI artifact.
design: Extend .github/workflows/release.yaml after helm push: cosign keyless signing (OIDC via GitHub Actions id-token: write) of the pushed OCI chart digest — 'cosign sign <ghcr-ref>@<digest>'. Document verification in README ('cosign verify --certificate-identity-regexp ... --certificate-oidc-issuer https://token.actions.githubusercontent.com'). Optionally also generate provenance/SLSA attestation (cosign attest or actions/attest-build-provenance). Keyless avoids secret management.
acceptance_criteria: Release workflow signs the pushed chart; README Releasing documents cosign verify for consumers and drops the 'future work' caveat.

title: Duplicate imagePullSecrets when listed in both global.imagePullSecrets and image.pullSecrets
description: platform-library/templates/_helpers.tpl:194-206 (pod template) appends global.imagePullSecrets and image.pullSecrets into one list with no dedup, so a secret named in both renders twice under imagePullSecrets. Kubernetes tolerates duplicates, but the manifest is wrong-looking, diffs noisily, and some GitOps validators flag it.
design: Dedupe before rendering: build the merged list then pass through 'uniq' (sprig) — e.g. '{{- $pullSecrets = $pullSecrets | uniq -}}' after the two range/append blocks. Entries are plain secret-name strings in both values paths, so uniq is safe. Cover with a render test where the same name appears in both lists.
acceptance_criteria: Same secret in both values paths renders once; distinct secrets from both paths all render; goldens updated if any fixture hits this; CORE.md Known Issues row removed.

title: Unknown workload.type silently falls back to Deployment — fail in template
description: platform-library/templates/_helpers.tpl:440-448 (platform.workload) dispatches on .Values.workload.type with eq StatefulSet / eq DaemonSet and a bare else that renders a Deployment. Consumers rendering WITHOUT the values schema (values.schema.json rejects unknown types for scaffolded charts, but library consumers may not copy it) who typo e.g. 'Statefulset' or 'CronJob' silently get a Deployment — wrong workload semantics with no error. CORE.md Known Issues notes the schema mitigation but the template itself should be defensive.
design: Replace the bare else with an explicit 'else if eq .Values.workload.type "Deployment"' branch plus a final else that calls fail with a message like: 'unknown workload.type %q — expected Deployment, StatefulSet or DaemonSet'. Keep default Deployment when workload.type is empty/unset to preserve backward compatibility for consumers who never set it (check values.yaml default first).
acceptance_criteria: helm template with workload.type=Bogus fails with a clear message; unset/empty and the three valid types render exactly as before (goldens unchanged); CORE.md Known Issues row updated.

title: kubeconform matrix legs should validate per-version renders
description: Judge follow-up wave 3: kubeconform loop validates the k8s-1.31 render against 1.32-1.36 schemas; if apiVersion negotiation ever diverges by kube version, wrong doc gets validated. Re-render per version or comment the assumption.
close_reason: lint-library.sh kubeconform moved into per-version render loop; validates $out per --kube-version instead of canonical $raw. Gate ==> PASS, shellcheck clean, no runtime regression (1:55 vs 1:58 baseline). Implemented by Sonnet executor via operating layer, reviewed by Fable.

title: Pin datreeio CRDs-catalog to commit SHA in lint-library.sh
description: Judge follow-up wave 3: kubeconform -schema-location points at CRDs-catalog main branch — mutable upstream can break/reinterpret CI. Pin to a commit SHA with documented bump procedure. Also consider SHA-pinning actions/checkout.
close_reason: CRD_CATALOG_REF pin added to lint-library.sh; full CI-strict gate ==> PASS with pinned URL (fresh downloads), shellcheck clean

title: P2-15: Rewrite CORE.md and purge v1 doc artifacts
description: CORE.md:111-142 stale directory listing/nonexistent templates; :144-217 consumer integration entirely v1; :88-97 stale known-issue lines; root configuration.yaml is v1 artifact. Fix: rewrite structure/integration/known-issues against v2; examples match schema enums; regenerate or delete configuration.yaml. (fable5-review.md #15)
close_reason: Implemented via relay on relay/08cdd64-release-docs, judge PASS 0.93

title: P2-13: new-app-chart.sh argument validation (YAML injection)
description: --repo/--version/--app-version interpolated into generated Chart.yaml heredoc unvalidated (new-app-chart.sh:56-72); newline-bearing values inject YAML keys. Fix: validate version/app-version against semver charset; repo against scheme allowlist (oci://, https://, file://); reject control chars/newlines; fix SC2015 at line 44; guard jq in lint-library.sh:25. (fable5-review.md #13)
close_reason: Implemented via relay on relay/6d1fa3a-schema-scaffold, judge PASS 0.94

title: P2-14: Release automation (OCI publish, CHANGELOG, semver tags)
description: Docs advertise oci:// but no packaging script, CHANGELOG, tags, or release automation; scaffold default repo is file://../platform-library. Fix: tag-triggered release workflow (helm package + push to OCI, optional cosign), CHANGELOG, semver tags matching Chart.yaml. (fable5-review.md #14)
close_reason: Implemented via relay on relay/08cdd64-release-docs, judge PASS 0.93

title: P2-12: Golden-file test snapshots + fixture coverage gaps
description: lint-library.sh:30-35 counts kinds without expectations; full fixture misses StatefulSet, DaemonSet, hook Jobs, persistence, configMap, secret, tlsSelfSigned, probes, initContainers, sidecars. Fix: golden-file snapshots (committed expected output, diff in CI) or per-fixture expected-kind-count assertions; add fixtures/--set legs for missing features; stop swallowing helm dependency update errors in tests/render.sh:11. (fable5-review.md #12)
close_reason: Implemented via relay on relay/e86a7cc-ci-golden-tests, judge PASS 0.95

title: P2-11: Add CI workflow (shellcheck, helm lint, render matrix, kubeconform required)
description: No .github/ at all. Fix: PR workflow running shellcheck on scripts+tests/render.sh; helm lint platform-library/; lint-library.sh render matrix across kube 1.31-1.36 and api-versions sets; kubeconform as required step across matrix with -schema-location for CRDs (cert-manager, Gateway API, Prom Operator, Istio); tag-triggered package+OCI push hooks into #14. (fable5-review.md #11)
close_reason: Implemented via relay on relay/e86a7cc-ci-golden-tests, judge PASS 0.95

title: Remove dead code and reconcile undeclared/dead values keys
description: Confirmed zero-caller helpers: platform.util.merge (_util.tpl:31 — yet CORE.md:21,69, .claude/skills/template-house-style/SKILL.md:13 and .claude/operating/discovery.md:39 all document invariants about it, i.e. a documented contract around a function nothing calls), platform.service.endpoint (_helpers.tpl:472), global.subchartEndpoint (_helpers.tpl:483 — also crashes on a subchart whose values lack a service key, unguarded index at :490), global.allEndpoints (_helpers.tpl:560, a pure alias kept for 'backward compatibility' in an unreleased chart). Unreachable branches: _hpa.yaml:4-6 (empty else), _app.yaml:70 (gate is 'or serviceAccount.create serviceAccount.name' but platform.serviceAccount renders only if .create, so the .name half can never emit), _service.yaml:47-49 (empty-ports fallback; values.yaml:69 always supplies one), _pdb.yaml:16-17 (maxUnavailable branch unreachable because minAvailable defaults to 1 — verified: --set podDisruptionBudget.maxUnavailable=1 still renders minAvailable: 1). Dead values keys (declared, never read): global.storageClass (values.yaml:24), serviceAccount.labels (:471), cronJob.sidecars (:406). Undeclared values keys (read by templates, absent from values.yaml AND the schema, working only because additionalProperties: true): topologySpreadConstraints (_helpers.tpl:306), podDisruptionBudget.maxUnavailable (_pdb.yaml:16), daemonSet.tolerations (_helpers.tpl:334, used by the daemon fixture).
design: Note the two halves pull in opposite directions: dead keys should be DELETED, undeclared-but-working keys should be DECLARED (they are real features a consumer cannot discover by reading the values contract). Do both. Much of the dead-helper list overlaps the serviceEndpoints deletion — sequence that first, then sweep the remainder. Also fix the PDB minAvailable/maxUnavailable precedence so maxUnavailable is actually selectable.
acceptance_criteria: Every listed helper/branch removed or made reachable; every dead values key deleted; every undeclared key added to values.yaml and the schema; goldens unchanged (proving all of it really was dead); the docs referencing platform.util.merge updated.
notes:
CORRECTION (2026-07-13): the DESCRIPTION is wrong about _service.yaml:47-49. That empty-ports fallback is NOT dead code. values.yaml:69 supplies a default port, but Go templates treat an empty list as falsey, so any consumer setting 'service.ports: []' takes the fallback and gets the primary port synthesized from platform.primaryServicePort. Verified by reading _service.yaml:46-50. Left in place deliberately; do not remove.

Also note _pdb.yaml no longer has the minAvailable/maxUnavailable precedence bug described here - it was fixed in an earlier commit on this branch and now fails closed when both are set. Verified: 'tests/render.sh minimal --set podDisruptionBudget.enabled=true --set podDisruptionBudget.maxUnavailable=1' renders 'maxUnavailable: 1'.

FINAL ITEM CLOSED: the three undeclared keys (root topologySpreadConstraints, daemonSet.tolerations, podDisruptionBudget.maxUnavailable) are now typed in values.schema.reference.json, plus podDisruptionBudget.minAvailable as its mutually exclusive sibling. Proven to bite: '--set topologySpreadConstraints=notalist' and '--set-json daemonSet.tolerations="nope"' are both rejected by the consumer-side values.schema.json at render time. Goldens unchanged (schema-only change, no render change). Gate: REQUIRE_KUBECONFORM=1 REQUIRE_CHECK_JSONSCHEMA=1 scripts/lint-library.sh => PASS.
close_reason: Final item done: the three undeclared values keys are now typed in values.schema.reference.json. All other acceptance items landed in earlier commits on fix/p1-render-correctness. The _service.yaml empty-ports item in the description was a misdiagnosis (see notes) and is intentionally not actioned.

title: Document --api-versions gv/gvk asymmetry in README
description: Discovered during the 2026-07-10 handover review: the library's capability gate checks .Capabilities.APIVersions.Has with the full group/version/Kind string, so helm's CLI flag --api-versions group/version silently fails to enable CRD-backed Kinds (verified: --api-versions monitoring.coreos.com/v1 renders no ServiceMonitor; monitoring.coreos.com/v1/ServiceMonitor does). Only the values force-assume list (capabilities.apiVersions) matches the bare group/version form. This sharp edge is documented in .claude/skills/capability-gates and vault/concepts/template-vs-cluster-capabilities.md but not in the repo's README, where consumers will actually look. Add a short note to the README capabilities section.
close_reason: README capability-gating section corrected: bare group/version CLI form documented as non-working; values-list form noted as the one accepting bare group/version

title: Keep consumer values.schema.json in sync with the library version (schema distribution)
description: The values contract schema is copied into each consumer chart once at scaffold time (new-app-chart.sh, spec §285-302) and never refreshed: a consumer that bumps its platform dependency to a version with new/changed keys keeps validating against the old schema — silent under-validation that erodes the guarantee the copy exists to provide.
design: Spike then implement: options are (a) publish values.schema.reference.json as a release asset/OCI artifact alongside the chart and add 'new-app-chart.sh --update-schema' that fetches the copy matching the pinned library version; (b) a consumer-side CI check comparing the local values.schema.json hash against the schema shipped in the resolved chart dependency (charts/platform-*.tgz contains the reference), failing with update instructions; (c) document a manual refresh step in the migration/upgrade docs. (b) is lowest-friction and works offline from the already-vendored dependency tarball; recommend (b) + a helper flag in new-app-chart.sh for the refresh itself. Any auto-sync must respect Helm's schema-lives-in-chart-dir model.
acceptance_criteria: A documented, tool-assisted path exists to detect and refresh a stale consumer schema after a library version bump; scaffold README/template mentions it; ideally a copy-pasteable CI snippet ships in docs.

title: Docs accuracy pass: spec fixture undercount, extraManifests separator claim, README reference gaps
description: (1) docs/specs/platform-library-v2-architecture.md §7a/§8 (:330-372) claims the render matrix covers 'minimal and full' — the suite actually runs four fixtures (minimal full stateful daemon, scripts/lint-library.sh:30) each with a committed golden; a contributor trusting the spec misses regenerating stateful/daemon goldens. (2) Spec §2.3 (:95-99) claims extraManifests applies the same 'separator only when non-empty' guard as extraObjects; the code emits --- unconditionally per item (_util.tpl:117-124) so a string manifest that tpl-renders to empty yields a stray empty document — verified by read; either fix the doc or add the trim+guard to match the contract (guard preferred: also extend the lint-library.sh empty-doc assertion to this path). (3) README gaps: rendered-but-undocumented values updateStrategy (values.yaml:107 → _deployment.yaml:32-33), schedulerName, podRestartPolicy, hostAliases (values.yaml:494-497); service.type docs omit ExternalName which the schema enum allows (values.schema.reference.json:35 vs README:179); README claims open-ended 'Helm 4.0+' while the spec/PRD scope 4.0-4.2 verified 4.2.0 — add a short chart-version ↔ K8s/Helm compatibility table for OCI consumers.
design: One docs-focused PR: update spec §7a/§8 fixture list; pick guard-the-code for extraManifests (trim rendered string, skip empty, matching extraObjects _util.tpl:101-105) since the spec states the desirable contract — plus doc note; add missing values to README config reference; align service.type enum; add compatibility table sourced from the PRD support matrix. Code change is ~5 lines in _util.tpl with golden-neutral behavior for all existing fixtures (none render empty extraManifests).
acceptance_criteria: Spec matches the four-fixture reality; extraManifests cannot emit an empty document (assertion extended); README documents all rendered values, correct service.type enum, and a compatibility table.

title: Deduplicate pod-spec and workload-metadata template logic (triplicated across workload/CronJob/hook-Job)
description: Pod hardening logic (serviceAccountName, automountServiceAccountToken, enableServiceLinks, podSecurityContext, pull-secret aggregation, container securityContext) is implemented three times: platform.podTemplateSpec (_helpers.tpl:190-357), _cronjob.yaml:50-97, and platform.renderHookJob (_helpers.tpl:735-763). They have already drifted: CronJob/hook pods never receive affinity/topologySpreadConstraints/priorityClassName (CronJob has its own nodeSelector/tolerations keys but no affinity), and user-supplied cronJob.containers bypass containerSecurityContext entirely — while README claims the security contexts apply to workload, CronJob, AND hook Jobs. Separately the metadata: header (name/namespace/labels/annotations) is copy-pasted verbatim across _deployment.yaml:4-23, _statefulset.yaml:4-23, _daemonset.yaml:4-23. Also: platform.util.merge (_util.tpl:31-36) is defined and documented as public API (CORE.md:69, spec §) but has zero call sites and zero fixture coverage — decide to exercise or drop. _helpers.tpl at 763 lines also buries the 168-line hook-Job generator that the docs imply lives in _job-*.yaml.
design: Render-neutral refactor protected by goldens: (1) extract a 'pod hardening preamble' helper (SA/automount/enableServiceLinks/podSecurityContext/pullSecrets) consumed by podTemplateSpec, _cronjob.yaml and renderHookJob — keep container-shape differences local; (2) extract platform.workloadMetadata for the three workload metadata headers; (3) move renderHookJob into a _hook-job.tpl (or the _job-*.yaml files) and umbrella endpoint helpers (_helpers.tpl:472-591) into _endpoints.tpl so _helpers.tpl shrinks to naming/labels/image/pod-template; (4) for platform.util.merge either add a fixture that overrides a tier-1 template through it (locking the documented invariant behind a golden) or delete it and its docs. Any intentional behavior alignment discovered while deduping (e.g. giving hook pods affinity) is a SEPARATE bead — this one must keep goldens byte-identical.
acceptance_criteria: lint-library.sh passes with UNCHANGED goldens (byte-identical); pod hardening defined once; _helpers.tpl reduced to its documented scope; platform.util.merge either fixture-covered or removed.

title: lint-library.sh: overridable FIXTURES/KUBE_VERSIONS for a fast local feedback loop
description: The only test entry point runs 4 fixtures x 6 k8s versions twice (render matrix + kubeconform, scripts/lint-library.sh:30-31,150-161) with network-fetching CRD schemas — every template edit pays the full matrix to get any signal, discouraging local runs before push. No Makefile/justfile target exists.
design: Make the FIXTURES and KUBE_VERSIONS arrays overridable from the environment (e.g. FIXTURES="${FIXTURES_OVERRIDE:-minimal full stateful daemon}" pattern or read -ra from an env var), keeping today's full matrix as the default so CI is unchanged. Document a quick invocation in CORE.md/README (FIXTURES=full KUBE_VERSIONS=1.31 scripts/lint-library.sh). Optionally add a Makefile with make test (full) and make quick targets. Goldens: skip golden UPDATE in quick mode or leave semantics as-is (diff only runs for selected fixtures).
acceptance_criteria: FIXTURES=full KUBE_VERSIONS=1.31 scripts/lint-library.sh completes in a fraction of the full-matrix time and runs only that slice; bare invocation identical to today; documented.

title: Prune dead apiVersion fallbacks in the capability registry (or document why they stay)
description: platform-library/templates/_capabilities.tpl keeps fallback apiVersions that can never be served on the supported Kubernetes range 1.31–1.36 (README: 'Targets Kubernetes 1.31–1.36'): autoscaling/v2beta1 + v2beta2 HPA (removed 1.26/1.25), extensions/v1beta1 and networking.k8s.io/v1beta1 Ingress (removed 1.22/1.16), batch/v1beta1 CronJob (removed 1.25), policy/v1beta1 PDB (removed 1.25). Lines ~92-98. They are dead code on any supported cluster and imply the chart might emit ancient apiVersions. (CRD-backed fallbacks like Istio/Gateway API v1beta1 are DIFFERENT — CRD-install-dependent, keep those.)
design: Either prune the built-in k8s dead fallbacks from the registry (they can only match if an operator force-assumes them, which the force-assume mechanism could still allow explicitly), or add a comment block in _capabilities.tpl documenting that they exist solely for force-assume/older-cluster escape hatches. Decide one way and make the registry match the declared support matrix. Check tests/lint-library.sh matrix for anything that exercises these fallbacks before removing.
acceptance_criteria: No built-in-API fallback in the registry predates what K8s 1.31 serves, OR a registry comment explains the retention policy; lint/goldens/kubeconform matrix still green.
close_reason: Fixed by 9c34617 — dead pre-floor apiVersion fallbacks pruned from _capabilities.tpl; remaining v1beta1 entries (Istio, Gateway API) are live CRD fallbacks, not dead.

title: Probe render condition is subtle and CORE.md mis-describes it — document or simplify
description: platform-library/templates/_helpers.tpl:244-251 gates each probe with 'and $ctx.Values.<probe>.enabled (omit $ctx.Values.<probe> "enabled")'. CORE.md 'Known Issues' claims omit returns an always-truthy dict so the condition reduces to enabled — that is WRONG: empty dicts are falsy in Go templates (verified with helm template: 'if (omit (dict "enabled" true) "enabled")' renders the else branch). The omit guard actually skips rendering an empty probe when a user sets only enabled: true with no other probe fields. So the code works and is not redundant, but the intent is undocumented and the CORE.md entry is factually incorrect.
design: Two-part cleanup: (1) fix the CORE.md Known Issues row (remove or rewrite — the condition is a deliberate empty-probe guard, not redundancy); (2) add a one-line template comment at _helpers.tpl:244 stating the omit guard prevents rendering an empty probe body. Optionally extract a named helper for readability. No behavior change.
acceptance_criteria: CORE.md no longer claims the condition is redundant; template comment explains the guard; rendered output unchanged (goldens identical).

title: tlsSelfSigned: rotate on near-expiry (compare NotAfter)
description: Judge follow-up from relay wave 2: lookup-reuse now never rotates, even after the 365-day cert expires. Add a NotAfter/renewBefore check that regenerates when within renewal window.
close_reason: Rotation via platform/tls-not-after annotation + renewBeforeDays values key (schema/README/CHANGELOG). Implemented by Sonnet executor (template/values/docs), completed by Fable (UPDATE_GOLDEN + final gate — executor idled before those steps). Gate ==> PASS twice, golden diff = one redacted-annotation hunk, shellcheck + metaschema clean. On-cluster lookup/rotation path unverified (no cluster) — source-reasoned only.

title: Guard mutual exclusion of certificate and tlsSelfSigned
description: Judge follow-up: both target Secret <fullname>-tls; enabling both collides (pre-existing). Add a fail/guard when certificate.enabled and tlsSelfSigned.enabled are both true.
close_reason: Composition-level fail guard in _app.yaml + negative test in lint-library.sh + README/CHANGELOG. Gate ==> PASS, goldens byte-identical, shellcheck clean. Implemented by Sonnet executor, reviewed by Fable (secretName targets verified at _certificate.yaml:31 / _tls-selfsigned.yaml:3).

title: new-app-chart.sh: enforce 63-char DNS label limit on chart name
description: scripts/new-app-chart.sh:49 validates the chart name charset against RFC 1123 ([a-z0-9]([a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9])?) but not length. Names over 63 chars scaffold fine but every rendered object name breaks at apply time in-cluster — failure surfaces at the worst point. Resource-name suffixes (-preinstall-script etc.) consume extra budget, so even ~50-char names can overflow for some Kinds.
design: Add a length check next to the existing regex: reject names over 63 and warn (or reject) above ~45 to leave suffix headroom (document the chosen bound in the error message). One-line check + shellcheck-clean error path; add a negative example to the script's usage comment.
acceptance_criteria: Scaffold rejects a 64-char name with a clear message; a ~50-char name warns about suffix headroom; existing valid names unaffected.
